Handles POST/REQUEST data without nonce verification

Potential undefined index: $assoc_args['page'] and $assoc_args['per_page'] and $assoc_args['post_type'] are accessed without isset() checks before use — if the keys are not set (e.g. user did not pass them), this will produce PHP notices/errors. The isset() guards only validate the value when present, but the subsequent unconditional access assumes the keys always exist.

Exception is caught and WP_CLI::error() is called, but $result is used after the catch block without any check — if the exception was thrown, $result is undefined, leading to a fatal 'variable undefined' error before WP_CLI::error() can terminate execution (WP_CLI::error() does call exit, but only if the $exit parameter is true, which is default; however the code after the try/catch still references $result unconditionally, which is a logic flaw regardless).
Code quality critical failure: swallowed exception details (original exception message discarded), and undefined variable usage after exception path.
